page: Microsoft Azure FX8ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity is 168.0 GB, yielding 21.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities. The server achieves a top-tier rating in Geekbench single-core testing with a score of 2362.0, and a top-tier single-threaded gzip compression speed of 34.9 GB/sec. Text processing performance is also in the top tier at 2996.0. Multi-core and multi-threaded benchmarks, such as the Geekbench multi-core score of 8997.0 and stress-ng multi-core performance of 8875.75, fall into the average category. Large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks show read speeds of 65.71 GB/sec and write speeds of 45.84 GB/sec. For language model workloads, the instance achieves a strong text generation speed of 291.75 tokens/sec on a small 135M model, but drops to a weak 8.45 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX8ms_v2 offers high memory density relative to its vCPU count, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, requiring external storage attachments for persistent data. This server is configured for workloads such as memory-intensive databases, caching layers like Redis, in-memory analytics, and single-threaded or memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX8ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-intensive virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with a 4.2 GHz clock speed and a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 168.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 21.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core execution, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2362.0 and a gzip compression speed of 34.9 GB/sec, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth fall into average ranges. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and enterprise applications requiring high memory capacity per core without massive parallel compute requirements.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX8ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 168 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, optimized for memory-heavy database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring high memory-to-core density and strong single-core performance for demanding database and caching workloads.

168.0 GB of RAM providing a high density of 21.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression benchmarks

Suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and in-memory analytics
